pick.rationale: UConn enters as the 2-seed with a superior 29-5 record and wins over elite competition including Arizona, Florida, Illinois, and Kansas, while UCLA's 23-11 mark came against a challenging Big Ten schedule but with more losses. Both teams carry significant injury concerns—UConn is without Jaylin Stewart (knee) and has Silas Demary Jr. dealing with an ankle issue, while UCLA's Tyler Bilodeau and Donovan Dent are expected to play but at less than full strength after injuries in the Big Ten tournament. UConn's concerning 72-52 blowout loss to St. John's in the Big East final raises legitimate upset questions, but Dan Hurley's tournament pedigree and the Huskies' overall body of work against top competition gives them the edge in this 2/7 matchup.

Jaylin Stewart has not played since Feb. 21, as the junior wing continues to recover from an unspecified knee injury, while Silas Demary Jr. was forced to exit Saturday's Big East Tournament final with a left ankle sprain . Dan Hurley provided updates on both players while speaking with the media Sunday evening. Advertisement Article continues below this ad "Health-wise, it will be close with Jaylin," Hurley said. The UConn coach provided a more promising update on his star point guard: "I think Silas is in pretty good shape." Jaylin Stewart has faced a mercurial junior season, starting UConn's first 10 games this season as freshman wing Braylon Mullins battled a pre-season injury — the Seattle native delivered double-digit scoring performances against Arizona and Texas. Stewart returned to an off-the-bench role once Mullins hit his stride around the New Year, but he stepped up with a crucial pair of 3-pointers against Villanova in his final game before the ongoing injury saga.
